content_units.main_claim: Vulca is a source-bound system that turns a commercial brief and selected source memory into a code-generated editable PPT deck.
content_units.business_logic: The product is credible only if the viewer can connect the real brief, allowed memory, generated editable output, and review boundary in one glance.

on_canvas_copy.headline: A real brief becomes editable PPT proof.
on_canvas_copy.supporting_line: Commercial brief, curated source memory, and code-generated editable PPT appear as one product path.

must_delete_from_canvas: decorative hero with no product object
delete_or_note_route.speaker_note: State that this is not an image-only demo; the product promise is editable PPT with inspectable inputs.

proof_object.business_logic: The product is credible only if the viewer can connect the real brief, allowed memory, generated editable output, and review boundary in one glance.
proof_object.why_this_proves_the_claim: The viewer sees the business input and editable output in one path, so the claim is not just a style promise.

layout_intent.first_read: A real brief becomes editable PPT proof.

chrome.purpose: Orient the scene without competing with the proof object.
Signal off-canvas detail without exposing raw records on the slide.

must_delete: decorative hero with no product object
layout_intent.scene_question: How can the scene show brief, memory, editable output, and review boundary as connected native objects?
layout_intent.rhythm_intent: Sparse first read: product path first, proof labels second.

design_moves: Scale and anchor the primary subject, then reduce nearby competitors.

content_units.main_claim: Allowed source observations are reduced into capability memory, message obligations, and proof objects before any slide is rendered.
content_units.business_logic: A deck can only explain its design decisions if source material is compiled into product memory and obligations before drawing starts.

on_canvas_copy.headline: Sources become product memory before drawing.
on_canvas_copy.supporting_line: Allowed source observations become capability memory, message obligations, and proof objects.

delete_or_note_route.speaker_note: Explain that tutorials and videos guide decisions only after they are converted into original product memory.

proof_object.business_logic: A deck can only explain its design decisions if source material is compiled into product memory and obligations before drawing starts.
proof_object.why_this_proves_the_claim: The slide shows the mechanism that turns source inputs into obligations, so design choices have upstream reasons.

layout_intent.first_read: Sources become product memory before drawing.

chrome.purpose: Orient the scene without competing with the proof object.
Signal off-canvas detail without exposing raw records on the slide.

layout_intent.scene_question: How can the scene show source to memory to obligation to proof without becoming a table?
layout_intent.rhythm_intent: Step through source, memory, obligation, and proof without becoming a table.

design_moves: Split dense content into ordered build steps.
Group repeated labels, keep one legend, and reveal detail labels only when needed.
Hide labels on the first build, then reveal them after primary visual focus is established.
Set motion paths and reveal order to follow the conceptual flow.

off_canvas_routes.speaker_note: Explain that tutorials and videos guide decisions only after they are converted into original product memory.

content_units.main_claim: Prompt-only can produce plausible slides, but it lacks source memory, proof routing, mapped design moves, and editable output guarantees.
content_units.business_logic: The product difference is credible when the alternate path fails at a named missing mechanism and the Vulca path continues to editable output.

on_canvas_copy.headline: The difference is mechanism, not taste.
on_canvas_copy.supporting_line: Vulca binds source memory, proof routing, design moves, and editable output before the deck is claimed.

delete_or_note_route.speaker_note: Name prompt-only and image-only paths as missing mechanism, not as inferior style by default.

proof_object.business_logic: The product difference is credible when the alternate path fails at a named missing mechanism and the Vulca path continues to editable output.
proof_object.why_this_proves_the_claim: The contrast is a system boundary, not a beauty contest.

layout_intent.first_read: The difference is mechanism, not taste.

chrome.purpose: Orient the scene without competing with the proof object.
Signal off-canvas detail without exposing raw records on the slide.

layout_intent.scene_question: How can the scene show the competing route breaking at missing mechanism while Vulca continues?
layout_intent.rhythm_intent: Use a matched before-after contrast so the product delta is the scene.

design_moves: Build a side-by-side or slide-pair comparison with matched scale and labels.
Delete or fade secondary boxes, borders, and card shells around non-primary content.
Remove card shells and redundant frames while preserving the content hierarchy.
Stagger callouts, arrows, and labels by small intervals after the anchor object.

off_canvas_routes.speaker_note: Name prompt-only and image-only paths as missing mechanism, not as inferior style by default.

content_units.main_claim: Each public proof marker points to capability binding, contract match, content check, and bad-control failure behind the surface.
content_units.business_logic: Trust improves when public proof is readable but still points to off-canvas records that can be inspected.

on_canvas_copy.headline: Proof stays inspectable without taking over the slide.
on_canvas_copy.supporting_line: Compact proof markers show that the workflow consumed content, contracts, and comparison boundaries.

delete_or_note_route.speaker_note: Explain that raw proof stays inspectable off-canvas so the public slide remains readable.

proof_object.business_logic: Trust improves when public proof is readable but still points to off-canvas records that can be inspected.
proof_object.why_this_proves_the_claim: The slide shows enough proof to trust the workflow while routing raw evidence to the viewer.

layout_intent.first_read: Proof stays inspectable without taking over the slide.

chrome.purpose: Orient the scene without competing with the proof object.

layout_intent.scene_question: How can four proof states be made visible as compact native objects with a viewer pointer?
layout_intent.rhythm_intent: Build from claim to proof object to route marker with one idea per beat.

design_moves: Scale and anchor the primary subject, then reduce nearby competitors.
Split dense content into ordered build steps.
Align blocks to shared edges and normalize gutters.
Animate the visual first, then fade in its label after a short delay.

content_units.main_claim: The brief, source memory, mapped design moves, code renderer, editable preview, and QA proof converge into one result object.
content_units.business_logic: The product becomes memorable when the user sees the operating loop converge into one editable artifact rather than disconnected process cards.

on_canvas_copy.headline: One editable result owns the product moment.
on_canvas_copy.supporting_line: Design memory, mapped design moves, code generation, and proof converge into one editable PPT preview.

delete_or_note_route.speaker_note: Keep this slide cinematic and least report-like; proof should orbit the result, not compete with it.

proof_object.business_logic: The product becomes memorable when the user sees the operating loop converge into one editable artifact rather than disconnected process cards.
proof_object.why_this_proves_the_claim: The product moment is a generated editable result with proof attached, not a set of disconnected diagrams.

layout_intent.first_read: One editable result owns the product moment.

chrome.purpose: Orient the scene without competing with the proof object.
Signal off-canvas detail without exposing raw records on the slide.

layout_intent.scene_question: How can the editable result dominate while proof remains attached and legible?
layout_intent.rhythm_intent: Hold the strongest proof object before adding labels or secondary evidence.

design_moves: Scale and anchor the primary subject, then reduce nearby competitors.
Constrain content to a safe area and reserve a clear empty zone.
Create an initial visual-only beat, then reveal explanation text.
Hold after revealing the main result before adding labels or secondary evidence.

off_canvas_routes.speaker_note: Keep this slide cinematic and least report-like; proof should orbit the result, not compete with it.

content_units.main_claim: The story can feed the next generated deck, but public release waits for visual review, native inspection, source-boundary review, and human approval.
content_units.business_logic: Credibility is preserved when the deck names the next proof and review gate instead of turning content readiness into a release claim.

on_canvas_copy.headline: The next proof is generated, not claimed.
on_canvas_copy.supporting_line: This story becomes input for the next generated deck, then waits for review before release.

delete_or_note_route.speaker_note: Name the remaining gate calmly: generated proof, visual review, native inspection, source-boundary review, and human approval.

proof_object.business_logic: Credibility is preserved when the deck names the next proof and review gate instead of turning content readiness into a release claim.
proof_object.why_this_proves_the_claim: The close is credible because it names the remaining proof instead of claiming release.

layout_intent.first_read: The next proof is generated, not claimed.

chrome.purpose: Orient the scene without competing with the proof object.
Signal off-canvas detail without exposing raw records on the slide.

layout_intent.scene_question: How can the scene show a calm blocked release gate with the next generated proof clearly named?
layout_intent.rhythm_intent: Resolve to release-bound handoff without implying public readiness.

design_moves: Duplicate the slide and change one state variable per duplicate.
Fade, blur, or remove low-value background decoration.
Bring primary subject and active annotation forward; push context, shadows, and background back.
Set motion paths and reveal order to follow the conceptual flow.

off_canvas_routes.speaker_note: Name the remaining gate calmly: generated proof, visual review, native inspection, source-boundary review, and human approval.
